Title: Invoice totals wrong when line-item quantity is updated concurrently

# Investigation: Concurrent line-item edits cause lost updates and stale invoice total

**Issue**: #214 (https://github.com/acme/invoicely-saas/issues/214)
**Type**: BUG
**Investigated**: 2026-06-08T15:40:00Z

### Assessment
| Metric | Value | Reasoning |
|---|---|---|
| Severity | HIGH | Corrupts billed totals in production for ~12 paying users — money is wrong. |
| Complexity | MEDIUM | Fix is localized to one controller + a recompute query, but needs a transaction and optimistic lock. |
| Confidence | HIGH | Reproduced the read-modify-write gap directly in invoiceController.ts:88-104. |

## Problem Statement
PATCH /invoices/:id computes the invoice total from the request body's line items only, ignoring line items it didn't receive. Concurrent partial edits therefore clobber each other and the persisted total reflects just the last writer.

## Analysis
### Root Cause (5 Whys)
1. Why is the total wrong? Because it is computed from req.body.lineItems alone. `// SOURCE: src/api/controllers/invoiceController.ts:92`
```ts
const total = req.body.lineItems.reduce((s, li) => s + li.unitPriceCents * li.quantity, 0);
```
2. Why does that lose data? The request only carries the items the user touched; untouched persisted items are excluded from the sum.
3. Why aren't persisted items re-read? The handler does `prisma.invoice.update` with the body total — no post-write SELECT of all line items. `// SOURCE: src/api/controllers/invoiceController.ts:98`
4. Why do concurrent saves overwrite? No version/updatedAt check — last write wins. There is no `version` column on Invoice (prisma/schema.prisma:44-60).
5. Root cause: total is derived from partial input instead of from the full persisted line-item set, with no optimistic-concurrency guard.

### Affected Files
| File | Lines | Action | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| src/api/controllers/invoiceController.ts | 88-104 | UPDATE | Recompute total from persisted items in a tx |
| prisma/schema.prisma | 44-60 | UPDATE | Add `version Int @default(0)` to Invoice |
| db/migrations/<ts>_invoice_version/migration.sql | new | CREATE | Add version column |

### Integration Points
- Mailer/PDF read `invoice.totalCents`; once recompute is correct they need no change.

### Git History
- Introduced: commit a91c2e ("feat: editable line items"), 2026-02-11. Last modified: 4f8810d. Implication: bug has shipped since editable line items launched.

## Implementation Plan
### Step 1: Add optimistic-lock column
- File prisma/schema.prisma, Lines 44-60, Action UPDATE
- Required change: add `version Int @default(0)` to model Invoice; run `prisma migrate dev`.
- Why: detect concurrent writers and reject stale saves.

### Step 2: Recompute total from persisted items in a transaction
- File src/api/controllers/invoiceController.ts, Lines 88-104, Action UPDATE
- Current code:
```ts
const total = req.body.lineItems.reduce((s, li) => s + li.unitPriceCents * li.quantity, 0);
await prisma.invoice.update({ where: { id, tenantId: req.tenantId }, data: { totalCents: total } });
```
- Required change:
```ts
await prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => {
  const current = await tx.invoice.findFirstOrThrow({ where: { id, tenantId: req.tenantId } });
  if (req.body.version !== current.version) throw new ConflictError('invoice modified by another request');
  await tx.lineItem.updateMany... // apply only the changed items
  const items = await tx.lineItem.findMany({ where: { invoiceId: id } });
  const totalCents = items.reduce((s, li) => s + li.unitPriceCents * li.quantity, 0);
  await tx.invoice.update({ where: { id, tenantId: req.tenantId }, data: { totalCents, version: { increment: 1 } } });
});
```
- Why: total is now derived from the full persisted set; version check turns lost updates into a 409.

### Step 3: Add/Update Tests
```
describe('PATCH /invoices/:id concurrency', () => {
  it('recomputes total from all persisted line items, not just the body');
  it('returns 409 when version is stale');
  it('keeps both edits when applied sequentially with refreshed version');
});
```

## Patterns to Follow
```ts
// SOURCE: src/api/controllers/invoiceController.ts:31 — zod + tenant scoping
if (!parsed.success) return res.status(400).json({ error: parsed.error.flatten() });
```

## Edge Cases & Risks
| Risk/Edge Case | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Client omits version | Treat as stale -> 409, force refetch |
| Item deleted by other writer | findMany after update reflects truth |
| No raw SQL allowed | Use Prisma tx + updateMany only |

## Validation
```bash
npx tsc --noEmit
npx jest invoice
npx prisma migrate dev --name invoice_version
```
Manual: open two tabs, edit different items, save both — both persist; second stale save returns 409.

## Scope Boundaries
IN SCOPE: total recompute, optimistic lock, tests.
OUT OF SCOPE: real-time multi-tab sync UI, websockets.

## Metadata
Investigated by: archon-investigate-issue · 2026-06-08T15:40:00Z · /artifacts/wf_lostupdate_309/investigation.md

# Investigation: Presence endpoint 500s on transient Redis unavailability

**Issue**: #412 (https://github.com/wayfarer/wayfarer-collab-api/issues/412)
**Type**: BUG
**Investigated**: 2026-06-08T21:20:11Z

### Assessment
| Metric | Value | Reasoning |
|--------|-------|-----------|
| Severity | HIGH | ~800 production 500s in 4 min during a routine maintenance window, with stack-trace leakage to clients. |
| Complexity | LOW | Single read path in src/routes/presence.ts:8-11; fix is a timeout + try/catch-free fallback via the existing error middleware. |
| Confidence | HIGH | Root cause reproduced from code: `redis.smembers` is awaited with no timeout and no degradation branch (presence.ts:9). |

## Problem Statement
GET /api/trips/:id/presence awaits `redis.smembers` with no command timeout and no fallback. When Redis is briefly unavailable the request hangs to the Node socket timeout and surfaces a raw Redis error, leaking stack traces and returning 500 instead of degrading gracefully.

## Analysis
### Root Cause (5 Whys)
- Why 500? The awaited Redis call rejects with a connection error. `// SOURCE: src/routes/presence.ts:9` `const members = await redis.smembers(\`presence:${tripId}\`)`
- Why does it leak a raw error? No timeout and no degradation branch; the rejection propagates to the default Express handler which serializes the stack. `// SOURCE: src/routes/presence.ts:8-11`
- Why no timeout? The shared client is created without `commandTimeout`. `// SOURCE: src/lib/redis.ts:4` `export const redis = new Redis(process.env.REDIS_URL!)`
- Why does it hang ~30s? Without a command timeout the promise waits on the Node socket default. `// SOURCE: src/lib/redis.ts:4`
- Root cause: presence reads treat Redis as a hard dependency with no timeout and no fallback — presence is inherently ephemeral and should degrade to empty.

### Affected Files
| File | Lines | Action | Description |
|------|-------|--------|-------------|
| src/lib/redis.ts | 4 | UPDATE | Add `commandTimeout` so calls fail fast |
| src/routes/presence.ts | 8-11 | UPDATE | Degrade to empty list + Retry-After on Redis failure |

### Integration Points
Shared `redis` client (src/lib/redis.ts) used by heartbeat and pub/sub — a global command timeout affects all consumers; chosen value (1500ms) is safely above normal latency.

### Git History
Introduced: commit a91c4f2 (initial presence route). Last modified: 3f0e1bb. Implication: the no-timeout pattern has existed since inception; other Redis reads share the risk but #412 is scoped to the presence GET.

## Implementation Plan
### Step 1: Add a command timeout to the shared client
- File: src/lib/redis.ts, Line 4, Action: UPDATE
- Current: `export const redis = new Redis(process.env.REDIS_URL!)`
- Required: `export const redis = new Redis(process.env.REDIS_URL!, { commandTimeout: 1500, maxRetriesPerRequest: 1 })`
- Why: bounds the hang so the request fails fast instead of waiting on the socket timeout.

### Step 2: Degrade gracefully in the presence read
- File: src/routes/presence.ts, Lines 8-11, Action: UPDATE
- Current: `const members = await redis.smembers(\`presence:${tripId}\`)` then `res.json({ members })`
- Required: wrap the read in a helper that, on a Redis error, logs and responds `res.set('Retry-After','5').json({ members: [] })`. Keep the route async; do not introduce per-route business try/catch beyond the degradation guard, and let any non-Redis error fall through to the central error middleware.
- Why: presence is ephemeral; an empty list + Retry-After is correct degradation and avoids leaking errors.

### Step 3: Add/Update Tests
- File: tests/integration/presence.test.ts
- `describe('GET presence degradation')`: `it('returns empty members + Retry-After when Redis command times out')` (mock client to reject), `it('returns 200 members under normal Redis')`.

## Patterns to Follow
`// SOURCE: src/middleware/error.ts:12` sanitized JSON error shape — reuse rather than emitting raw errors.
`// SOURCE: src/lib/log.ts:10` `log.warn({ tripId }, 'presence.redis_degraded')`.

## Edge Cases & Risks
| Risk/Edge Case | Mitigation |
|----------------|------------|
| 1500ms timeout too tight under load | value > p99 normal latency; tunable via env |
| Global timeout affects heartbeat writes | acceptable — writes already idempotent and retried client-side |
| Empty list masks a real outage | log.warn + metric on degradation path |

## Validation
```bash
npm run type-check
npm run test:integration -- presence
```
Manual: stop Redis locally, `curl /api/trips/t1/presence` → 200 `{ "members": [] }` with `Retry-After: 5` within ~1.5s, no stack trace.

## Scope Boundaries
IN SCOPE: presence GET timeout + degradation, shared client timeout, tests.
OUT OF SCOPE: heartbeat write resilience, presence caching redesign, circuit breaker (deferred).

Issue #482 — bug, analytics, rss, priority:high
Title: Feed download counter double-counts requests that include `Range` header (podcast app resumable downloads)
Body: Podcast apps (Apple Podcasts, Overcast) send HTTP Range requests to resume interrupted downloads or probe duration. Our counter increments once per HTTP request, not once per full-file download, so a single listener resuming registers as 2–4 downloads. Expected: one download per unique listener per episode per calendar day (dedup by subscriber_hash). Actual: every HTTP request including partial Range requests increments. Dashboard shows 3–5× inflated numbers. RFC 3005 compliance requires counting only full downloads or initial-range requests (byte-range starting at 0).

# Investigation: Download counter double-counts Range requests

**Issue**: #482 (https://github.com/acme/tapecast-feed/issues/482)
**Type**: BUG
**Investigated**: 2026-06-08T18:55:00Z

### Assessment
| Metric | Value | Reasoning |
|---|---|---|
| Severity | HIGH | Inflates the core analytics product 3–5×; priority:high label and customer-facing dashboards affected. |
| Complexity | LOW | Fix is localized to the audio route's countability check plus dedup confirmation in one repository. |
| Confidence | HIGH | Reproduced the logic gap directly in src/routes/feed.ts; Range handling is the single increment site. |

## Problem Statement
The audio endpoint counts a download on every HTTP request. Range requests used for resume/probing each increment the counter, so one listener is counted multiple times, inflating dashboards 3–5×.

## Analysis
### Root Cause (5 Whys)
1. Why inflated? Each request increments. 2. Why? The countability check does not exclude partial Range requests. `// src/routes/feed.ts` increments whenever the request is served. 3. Why aren't they excluded? There is no `Range`-header gate that restricts counting to full/initial-range (`bytes=0-`) requests. 4. Why does dedup not save us? subscriber_hash dedup is per (episode, hash, day), but a single resume produces the same hash within the same day — so the only inflation is when counting happens before the dedup gate, or counting partial ranges as separate countable events. 5. Root: countability is request-driven, not download-driven; RFC 3005 says count only `bytes=0-` (initial) or full downloads.
### Affected Files
| File | Lines | Action | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| src/routes/feed.ts | audio handler | UPDATE | add `bytes=0-` countability gate before recording |
| src/repositories/downloadRepository.ts | recordDownload | VERIFY | ensure unique (episodeId, subscriber_hash, day) constraint |
### Integration Points
Download count surfaces in subscriber analytics dashboard via downloadRepository aggregates.
### Git History
Introduced when audio streaming route added; Range support predates counter dedup, so counting was never Range-aware.

## Implementation Plan
### Step 1: Gate counting on initial-range only
- File src/routes/feed.ts, audio handler, Action UPDATE
- Required change: `const isCountable = !rangeHeader || rangeHeader.startsWith('bytes=0-');` (exact `bytes=0-` prefix, NOT `bytes=0` — `bytes=0-500` probes and `bytes=00-` must be excluded).
- Why: matches RFC 3005 — count full or initial-range downloads only.
### Step 2: Confirm dedup is the dedup authority
- File src/repositories/downloadRepository.ts, recordDownload
- Required change: rely on DB unique constraint on (episodeId, subscriber_hash, calendar_day); do not dedup in-memory.
- Why: in-memory caches leak and are wrong across days; the hash already encodes the day.
### Step 3: Add/Update Tests
- describe('audio download counting') it('counts a request with no Range header'); it('counts bytes=0- as initial-range download'); it('does NOT count bytes=500-999 partial'); it('does NOT count bytes=0-500 byte probe'); it('dedups same subscriber_hash same day').

## Patterns to Follow
`// SOURCE: src/routes/feed.ts` — countability computed from `request.headers['range']` then guarded `if (isCountable) { ...recordDownload }`.

## Edge Cases & Risks
| Risk/Edge Case | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| `bytes=0-500` probe counted | Require exact `bytes=0-` prefix; add explicit test |
| Multiple resumes same day | Rely on subscriber_hash day-scoped unique constraint |
| Missing salt → identical hashes | Out of scope here; tracked separately |

## Validation
`npx vitest run src/routes/feed.test.ts` (0). Manual: curl with `-H 'Range: bytes=500-999'` then verify count unchanged; `Range: bytes=0-` increments once.

## Scope Boundaries
IN SCOPE: Range-aware countability, dedup confirmation, tests. OUT OF SCOPE: salt validation, IP-source trust, schema redesign.

# Investigation: Delta sync duplicates/misses rows under client clock skew

**Issue**: #482 (https://github.com/acme/academytrack-lms-mobile/issues/482)
**Type**: BUG
**Investigated**: 2026-06-08T00:00:00Z

### Assessment
| Metric | Value | Reasoning |
|---|---|---|
| Severity | HIGH | Causes silent client-side data corruption (progress reset to zero) on all production builds (iOS 2.3.1, Android 2.3.0). |
| Complexity | MEDIUM | Localized to sync.service + controller + client merge, but changes the sync contract (cursor) requiring a client change. |
| Confidence | HIGH | Root cause is directly observable at sync.service.ts:48 (strict `>` on client-supplied time) and confirmed by client merge having no recency guard at merge.ts:30. |

## Problem Statement
Delta sync uses a strict `updatedAt > :since` filter where `:since` is a client-device timestamp. Clock skew and the off-by-one boundary cause rows to be re-returned (or missed) across syncs, and the client SQLite merge upserts by id with no recency check, so an older re-sent row overwrites newer local progress.

## Analysis
### Root Cause (5 Whys)
1. Why does progress reset? The client upserts an older lesson_progress row over a newer local one — `mobile/src/sync/merge.ts:30` upserts by id with no `updatedAt` comparison.
2. Why is an older row received? The delta endpoint re-returns already-synced rows.
3. Why re-returned? `:since` is the client clock; when it ticks backward / lags the server, rows previously synced satisfy `updatedAt > :since` again — `src/sync/sync.service.ts:48`.
4. Why is the client clock trusted? The controller takes `@Query('since')` verbatim and parses it — `src/sync/sync.controller.ts:22`; there is no server-issued cursor — return shape is `{ records }` only at `src/sync/sync.service.ts:61`.
5. Why no cursor (primitive)? Delta sync v1 (commit a91f3e2) modeled the watermark as a wall-clock timestamp instead of a server-authoritative monotonic cursor. The sound primitive is a server-generated watermark, not client time.

Current code (actual):
```ts
// src/sync/sync.service.ts:48
.where('lp.updatedAt > :since', { since })
```
```ts
// src/sync/sync.service.ts:61
return { records };
```

### Affected Files
| File | Lines | Action | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| src/sync/sync.service.ts | 48,61 | UPDATE | accept opaque token; return server `nextSyncToken` from MAX(updatedAt) at query time |
| src/sync/sync.controller.ts | 22 | UPDATE | accept `?since` as opaque token (not parsed as raw client date) |
| mobile/src/sync/merge.ts | 30 | UPDATE | upsert only when incoming.updatedAt >= local.updatedAt; persist returned token |

### Integration Points
Offline-first SQLite merge (Capacitor) consumes the response; all timestamps are UTC. The token must be opaque and server-issued.

### Git History
Introduced: a91f3e2 (2025-11-03, 'feat: delta sync v1'). Last modified: b22d8c1. Implication: the timestamp-watermark design is original to v1; fix is a contract upgrade, not a regression of a later change.

## Implementation Plan
### Step 1: Server returns and consumes a monotonic cursor
- File src/sync/sync.service.ts, Lines 48/61, Action UPDATE.
- Current:
```ts
.where('lp.updatedAt > :since', { since })
...
return { records };
```
- Required:
```ts
const since = token ? this.decodeToken(token) : new Date(0);
const qb = this.repo.createQueryBuilder('lp')
  .where('lp.userId = :userId', { userId })
  .andWhere('(lp.updatedAt > :ts OR (lp.updatedAt = :ts AND lp.id > :id))', { ts: since.ts, id: since.id })
  .orderBy('lp.updatedAt', 'ASC').addOrderBy('lp.id', 'ASC');
const records = await qb.getMany();
const last = records[records.length - 1];
const nextSyncToken = last ? this.encodeToken(last.updatedAt, last.id) : token;
return { records, nextSyncToken };
```
- Why: composite (updatedAt,id) keyset cursor is monotonic and skew-proof; ties no longer drop or duplicate.
### Step 2: Controller treats `since` as an opaque token
- File src/sync/sync.controller.ts, Line 22, UPDATE: pass `@Query('since') token?: string` straight through; do not `new Date(since)`.
### Step 3: Client recency-guarded merge + token persistence
- File mobile/src/sync/merge.ts, Line 30, UPDATE: only upsert when `incoming.updatedAt >= local.updatedAt`; store `response.nextSyncToken` as the next `since`.
### Step 4: Add/Update Tests

# Investigation: Tickets admitted after event end time

**Issue**: #214 (https://github.com/org/venueops-ticketing/issues/214)
**Type**: BUG
**Investigated**: 2026-06-08T14:40:00Z

### Assessment
| Metric | Value | Reasoning |
|---|---|---|
| Severity | CRITICAL | Expired tickets gain paid entry — direct revenue/security loss confirmed at the door. |
| Complexity | MEDIUM | Two coupled root causes (stale cache + wrong HMAC expiry) but both are localized. |
| Confidence | HIGH | Repro is deterministic and both faults are visible in validate-ticket.ts and the QR signer. |

## Problem Statement
The scanner admits tickets hours after `doors_close` when offline. The event record is cached in IndexedDB at door-open and never refreshed, and the QR HMAC expiry encodes purchase time, so neither the cached server check nor the offline signature check enforces event end.

## Analysis
### Root Cause (5 Whys)
1. Why admitted late? Time check passed. 2. Why pass? `event.doorsClose` came from a stale IndexedDB copy (src/lib/offline/event-cache.ts:31 caches once, no TTL). 3. Why stale used? Offline path never refreshes (event-cache.ts:48 returns cached on miss-network). 4. Why no offline guard? The only time bound on the hot path is the HMAC expiry, which uses purchase time (src/lib/crypto/sign-payload.ts:22 `exp: ticket.purchasedAt`). 5. Why purchase time? Original signer copied purchase timestamp as a generic exp — never tied to event end. Root cause: validity is anchored to the wrong timestamp in two layers.
### Affected Files
| File | Lines | Action | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| src/lib/crypto/sign-payload.ts | 22 | UPDATE | set `exp` to event.doorsClose, not purchasedAt |
| src/lib/scanner/validate-ticket.ts | 60 | UPDATE | compare with `<=` and prefer signed exp on offline path |
| src/lib/offline/event-cache.ts | 31,48 | UPDATE | add TTL + refresh-if-online |
### Integration Points
QR signer (sign-payload), scanner hot path (validate-ticket), offline cache (event-cache). Append-only scan_events unaffected.
### Git History
Introduced: a1b2c3d (initial signer). Last modified: e4f5a6b. Implication: every issued QR carries wrong exp; fix must re-sign or validate against event end regardless of payload exp.

## Implementation Plan
### Step 1: Anchor HMAC expiry to event end
- File src/lib/crypto/sign-payload.ts, Line 22, Action UPDATE
- Current: `exp: ticket.purchasedAt.getTime()`
- Required: `exp: event.doorsClose.getTime()`
- Why: offline validity must reject past event end without a DB lookup.
### Step 2: Enforce signed exp on hot path with inclusive bound
- File src/lib/scanner/validate-ticket.ts, Line 60, Action UPDATE
- Current: `if (event && Date.now() < event.doorsClose.getTime())`
- Required: reject when `Date.now() >= parsed.exp` (signed) before any cached-event branch; use `>=` so the exact end millisecond is EXPIRED.
- Why: removes dependence on possibly-stale cached event.
### Step 3: TTL + refresh on event cache
- File src/lib/offline/event-cache.ts, Lines 31/48, Action UPDATE
- Add cachedAt + 15m TTL; when online and stale, refetch before serving.
### Step 4: Add/Update Tests
- describe('validateTicket time validity'): it('rejects EXPIRED at exact doors_close millisecond'); it('rejects EXPIRED 6h after end while offline'); it('signs exp as event end not purchase time').

## Patterns to Follow
// SOURCE: validate-ticket.ts:9 — typed status union `return { status: 'EXPIRED' }`.
// SOURCE: scanEvents insert validate-ticket.ts:45 — append-only logging.

## Edge Cases & Risks
| Risk/Edge Case | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Already-issued QRs carry wrong exp | Step 2 validates against signed exp; consider re-issue or grace migration noted Out of Scope |
| Clock skew on offline device | Use signed exp (server-issued) not device clock for the bound |
| Cache refresh fails offline | Serve cached but still enforce signed exp |

## Validation
`npm run type-check` && `npm test -- validate-ticket` && manual: scan 6h post-end offline -> EXPIRED.

## Scope Boundaries
IN SCOPE: exp anchoring, inclusive time bound, cache TTL, tests. OUT OF SCOPE: bulk re-issue of historical QRs (separate migration), the unrelated dedup/atomicity defects in validate-ticket.ts.

# Investigation: Approval stage skipped when approver email equals submitter email

**Issue**: #214 (https://github.com/org/formvault/issues/214)
**Type**: BUG
**Investigated**: 2026-06-08T14:40:00Z

### Assessment
| Metric | Value | Reasoning |
|--------|-------|-----------|
| Severity | CRITICAL | An access-control gate is bypassed, advancing approvals without authorization — security/compliance impact on a p1 workflow. |
| Complexity | LOW | Single comparison in one function changes from email string to userId PK. |
| Confidence | HIGH | The faulty comparison is directly located at app/services/approval.py:47 and the model exposes both user_id and email. |

## Problem Statement
The approval router decides whether a submitter is also the stage approver by comparing email strings. Because email is not unique across SSO and local accounts, a matching email silently auto-advances the stage without an explicit approval action, bypassing required authorization.

## Analysis
### Root Cause
5 Whys:
1. Why does the stage skip? The router marks the stage approved when submitter == approver. (app/services/approval.py:45-49)
2. Why does it match wrongly? Equality is on `email`, not identity. `if submission.submitter_email == stage["approverEmail"]:` (app/services/approval.py:47)
3. Why is email used? Stage config stores `approverEmail` and the submitter carries an email field. (app/models.py: Submission.submitter_email)
4. Why is that unsafe? Email is non-unique across SSO/local accounts, so distinct users can share one address. (issue report; auth model)
5. Why is the PK available? Submissions reference `submitter_id` and approvers can be resolved to `user_id`. (app/models.py: Submission.submitter_id) — Root cause: identity comparison uses a non-unique attribute instead of the user PK.

### Affected Files
| File | Lines | Action | Description |
|------|-------|--------|-------------|
| app/services/approval.py | 45-49 | UPDATE | compare by user_id PK, not email |
| app/models.py | (Stage schema) | UPDATE | stage approver should carry approverUserId |
| tests/test_approval.py | new | CREATE | regression for shared-email case |

### Integration Points
- Approval stage config (ordered JSON array on form schema) must expose `approverUserId`.
- Submission creation path that triggers auto-advance.

### Git History
Introduced: commit a1b2c3 ("add approval auto-advance"). Last modified: same. Implication: every release since a1b2c3 has carried the bypass.

## Implementation Plan
### Step 1: Compare by user PK
- File: app/services/approval.py, Lines 45-49, Action: UPDATE
- Current code:
```python
first_stage = form.schema.get("approvalStages", [{}])[stage_index]
if submission.submitter_email == first_stage.get("approverEmail"):
    submission.status = f"stage_{stage_index+1}_approved"
```
- Required change:
```python
stage = form.schema.get("approvalStages", [{}])[stage_index]
if submission.submitter_id == stage.get("approverUserId"):
    submission.status = f"stage_{stage_index+1}_approved"
```
- Why: userId (DB PK) is unique; email is not. This restores the access-control invariant.

### Step 2: Stage schema carries approverUserId
- Ensure `approvalStages[*].approverUserId` is populated; reject stages missing it with {error, code} ({"error": "Stage approver missing userId", "code": "STAGE_APPROVER_INVALID"}).

### Step 3: Add/Update Tests
```python
# tests/test_approval.py
pytestmark = pytest.mark.anyio

async def test_shared_email_does_not_auto_advance(session):
    # submitter and approver share email but differ by user_id
    # assert status stays 'pending', no stage skip

async def test_same_user_id_auto_advances(session):
    # submitter_id == approverUserId -> stage approved
```

## Patterns to Follow
```python
# SOURCE: app/services/approval.py:30-33 — identity already available
submitter_id = submission.submitter_id  # DB PK, unique
```

## Edge Cases & Risks
| Risk/Edge Case | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Legacy stages without approverUserId | reject with STAGE_APPROVER_INVALID; migration to backfill |
| Approver == submitter legitimately (same person) | correct: same user_id auto-advances by design |
| Null submitter_id | treat as non-match; never auto-advance |

## Validation
```bash
pytest tests/test_approval.py -q
ruff check app && mypy app
```
Manual: create a form whose stage-2 approverUserId differs from submitter but shares email; submit; confirm status stays pending.

## Scope Boundaries
IN SCOPE: identity comparison fix, stage schema approverUserId, regression tests.
OUT OF SCOPE: redesign of approval routing, email-to-user resolution UI, SSO account merging.

# Investigation: 0-byte signed PDF for transcripts over 200KB

**Issue**: #482 (https://github.com/docpod/docpod-unified/issues/482)
**Type**: BUG
**Investigated**: 2026-06-08T14:40:00Z

### Assessment
| Metric | Value | Reasoning |
|---|---|---|
| Severity | HIGH | Silent data loss — compliance artifact is empty yet task reports SUCCESS, with no Sentry alert. |
| Complexity | LOW | Single missing seek(0)/getvalue fix in one render function. |
| Confidence | HIGH | Repro is deterministic at 200KB and the buffer read path is visible at pdf_render.py:41. |

## Problem Statement
For transcripts >=200KB the signing task stores a 0-byte PDF while reporting SUCCESS. The PDF buffer is read before the writer flushes/seeks, so on the large-input code path an empty buffer is uploaded to S3 and persisted.

## Analysis
### Root Cause (5 Whys)
1. Why empty PDF? `buf.read()` returns b'' — buffer position is at end. (app/services/pdf_render.py:41)
2. Why at end? canvas.save() writes then leaves the cursor at EOF; no seek(0). (pdf_render.py:38-41)
3. Why only >200KB? small inputs flush within one showPage; large inputs add pages and the early `buf.read()` runs before the final flush. (pdf_render.py:30-41)
4. Why no error? read() of an empty buffer is valid; upload of b'' succeeds. (app/services/storage.py:55)
5. Why no Sentry? task returns normally; no size assertion. (app/tasks/sign_transcript.py:27)
Root cause: missing `buf.seek(0)` (and absent non-empty guard) in pdf_render — a symptom of reading before flushing, not an S3 problem.

### Affected Files
| File | Lines | Action | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| app/services/pdf_render.py | 38-41 | UPDATE | seek(0) + use getvalue(); guard empty |
| app/tasks/sign_transcript.py | 25-29 | UPDATE | assert non-zero bytes before store |
| tests/services/test_pdf_render.py | new | CREATE | regression at 220KB |

### Integration Points
sign_transcript task -> pdf_render.render() -> signing.sign_pdf() -> storage.put().
### Git History
Introduced commit a91f4c2 (pdf_render initial); last modified 3f0e1b9; implication: bug present since feature landed, never triggered by short test fixtures.

## Implementation Plan
### Step 1: Fix buffer read in pdf_render
- File app/services/pdf_render.py, Lines 38-41, Action UPDATE
- Current code:
```python
canvas.save()
return buf.read()
```
- Required change:
```python
canvas.save()
buf.seek(0)
data = buf.getvalue()
if not data:
    raise ValueError('pdf_render produced empty buffer')
return data
```
- Why: positions buffer at start and fails loud instead of silently returning b''.

### Step 2: Guard in task before store
- File app/tasks/sign_transcript.py, Lines 25-29, Action UPDATE
- Current code:
```python
pdf = render(transcript, meta)
store(signed_path, sign_pdf(pdf, hsm_ref))
```
- Required change:
```python
pdf = render(transcript, meta)
if not pdf:
    raise ValueError(f'empty pdf for episode {episode_id}')
store(signed_path, sign_pdf(pdf, hsm_ref))
```
- Why: defense in depth; surfaces failure to Flower/Sentry.

### Step 3: Add/Update Tests
```python
describe('pdf_render large input'):
  it('returns non-empty bytes for a 220KB transcript')
  it('paginates beyond one page')
  it('raises on empty transcript')
```

## Patterns to Follow
```python
# SOURCE: app/services/notify.py:20-23 — structured logging on failure
logger.error('sign.empty_pdf', extra={'episode_id': episode_id})
```

## Edge Cases & Risks
| Risk/Edge Case | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Existing 0-byte PDFs already stored | follow-up backfill task (out of scope) |
| Empty transcript legitimately | raise + skip store, log |

## Validation
```bash
pytest tests/services/test_pdf_render.py -q
pytest tests/tasks/test_sign_transcript.py -q
```
Manual: publish a 220KB-transcript episode, GET signed-transcript, confirm Content-Length > 0.

## Scope Boundaries
IN SCOPE: seek(0) fix, task guard, regression tests.
OUT OF SCOPE: backfilling previously-stored empty PDFs; S3 lifecycle changes.

# Investigation: Webhook crashes on Vercel KV outage

**Issue**: #214 (https://github.com/podscout/podscout-analytics/issues/214)
**Type**: BUG
**Investigated**: 2026-06-08T18:45:00Z

### Assessment
| Metric | Value | Reasoning |
|---|---|---|
| Severity | HIGH | An unhandled rejection crashes the Node process, dropping every concurrent webhook, not just the affected one. |
| Complexity | LOW | Single function; wrap one await in try/catch with fail-open fallback. |
| Confidence | HIGH | Root cause reproduced directly from the unguarded `await kv.get` at the cited line. |

## Problem Statement
The webhook idempotency lookup calls `await kv.get(idempotencyKey)` with no error handling. When Vercel KV is down or rate-limited, the rejection escapes the handler before its error boundary, producing an UnhandledPromiseRejection that crashes the process.

## Analysis
### Root Cause (5 Whys)
1. Why crash? An unhandled promise rejection propagates. 
2. Why unhandled? `await kv.get(idempotencyKey)` (src/app/api/webhooks/[provider]/route.ts:34) has no try/catch.
3. Why not caught by the route boundary? The KV call runs in the handler body before the boundary wraps downstream processing.
4. Why no fallback? Idempotency was designed assuming KV is always reachable.
5. Root: KV is treated as a hard dependency for a check that should fail open.

Actual code (route.ts:33-36):
```ts
const idempotencyKey = `wh:${provider}:${event.id}`;
const seen = await kv.get(idempotencyKey);
if (seen) return new Response('duplicate', { status: 200 });
```

### Affected Files
| File | Lines | Action | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| src/app/api/webhooks/[provider]/route.ts | 33-44 | UPDATE | wrap KV read + write in try/catch, fail open |

### Integration Points
HMAC verification (route.ts:20-28) already runs before this — keep it ahead of the KV call. The KV write `kv.set(key, '1', { ex: 172800 })` (route.ts:42) must also be guarded.

### Git History
Introduced: commit a91f2c (feat: webhook idempotency, 2026-02). Last modified: same. Implication: never had failure handling.

## Implementation Plan
### Step 1: Fail-open KV read
- File: src/app/api/webhooks/[provider]/route.ts, Lines 34, Action: UPDATE
- Current:
```ts
const seen = await kv.get(idempotencyKey);
```
- Required:
```ts
let seen: unknown = null;
try {
  seen = await kv.get(idempotencyKey);
} catch (err) {
  console.error('[webhook] KV unavailable, processing without idempotency', { provider, err });
}
```
- Why: on KV failure, log and continue (accept duplicate-processing risk per expected behavior).

### Step 2: Guard the KV write
- File: route.ts, Lines 42, Action: UPDATE
- Current: `await kv.set(idempotencyKey, '1', { ex: 172800 });`
- Required: wrap in try/catch with the same console.error pattern; do not block the response on a write failure.
- Why: write failure during an outage must not crash or 500.

### Step 3: Add/Update Tests

# Investigation: Auth middleware allows expired JWT via X-Offline-Mode bypass

**Issue**: #214 (https://github.com/org/eventpass-mobile-api/issues/214)
**Type**: BUG
**Investigated**: 2026-06-08T15:55:00Z

### Assessment
| Metric | Value | Reasoning |
|---|---|---|
| Severity | CRITICAL | An attacker with any once-valid token can admit attendees indefinitely; auth.ts:28 short-circuits before jwt.verify, defeating the 15-min expiry guarantee. |
| Complexity | LOW | Single-line removal plus restoring verify order; one file, no schema/API change. |
| Confidence | HIGH | Reporter's file:line confirmed by exploration; bypass at auth.ts:28 precedes verify at auth.ts:33. |

## Problem Statement
The main auth middleware returns `next()` whenever the `X-Offline-Mode` header is present, skipping JWT verification entirely. This was meant for the batch route but was placed globally, so expired tokens are accepted on `POST /checkins`, admitting attendees after token expiry.

## Analysis
### Root Cause (5 Whys)
1. Why does an expired JWT succeed? Because verification never runs — `auth.ts:28 if (req.headers['x-offline-mode']) return next();` returns before line 33.
2. Why is the bypass there? Commit a91f4c2 'wip offline sync' added it to unblock offline batch work.
3. Why global? It was placed in `requireAuth` (mounted on all routes) instead of the batch route only.
4. Why undetected? `tests/integration/auth.test.ts` covers missing/invalid tokens but has no expired-token-with-offline-header case.
5. Why is offline-mode trusted at all for auth? A client-supplied header is not an authentication factor — the primitive (auth must validate token integrity+expiry) was violated.
Actual current code (src/middleware/auth.ts:24-34):
```ts
const token = (req.headers.authorization || '').replace('Bearer ', '');
if (!token) return res.status(401).json({ error: 'no_token' });
if (req.headers['x-offline-mode']) return next();   // line 28 — BUG
try {
  const claims = jwt.verify(token, secret);
  req.user = claims;
  return next();
```
### Affected Files
| File | Lines | Action | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| src/middleware/auth.ts | 28 | DELETE | remove global offline bypass |
| tests/integration/auth.test.ts | append | ADD | expired-token + X-Offline-Mode → 401 |

### Integration Points
Batch sync route still needs its legitimate flow — but it must use a normally-verified JWT (offline mode is a client cache concept, not an auth exemption). No route currently depends on the bypass for correctness.
### Git History
Introduced: a91f4c2 'wip offline sync' (2026-05-30). Last modified: a91f4c2. Implication: regression is ~9 days old; any token issued before expiry is currently exploitable.

## Implementation Plan
### Step 1: Remove the bypass
- File src/middleware/auth.ts, Line 28, Action DELETE
- Current code: `if (req.headers['x-offline-mode']) return next();`
- Required change: delete the line so flow falls through to `jwt.verify`.
- Why: header is attacker-controllable and must not gate authentication.
### Step 2: Add regression tests
- File tests/integration/auth.test.ts, Action ADD
```ts
describe('expired token', () => {
  it('returns 401 even with X-Offline-Mode header', async () => {
    const token = signExpired();
    await request(app).post('/api/v1/events/e1/checkins')
      .set('Authorization', `Bearer ${token}`)
      .set('X-Offline-Mode', '1').expect(401);
  });
});
```
- Why: locks the fix and documents intent.

## Patterns to Follow
// SOURCE: src/middleware/auth.ts:31-33 — verification via `jwt.verify(token, secret)` which throws `TokenExpiredError`; the existing catch returns 401, so deleting line 28 is sufficient.

## Edge Cases & Risks
| Risk/Edge Case | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Batch route relied on bypass for offline clients | Batch sync uses a valid (unexpired) JWT obtained while online; document this; verify batch tests still pass |
| Other routes set X-Offline-Mode | grep shows only checkin path; removal is safe |

## Validation
```bash
npm run type-check
npm test tests/integration/auth.test.ts
npm test
```
Manual: with an expired token and `X-Offline-Mode: 1`, `POST /checkins` returns 401.

## Scope Boundaries
IN SCOPE: delete auth.ts:28 bypass; add expired-token test.
OUT OF SCOPE: redesigning offline/batch auth; refresh-token rotation; rate limiting.

# Investigation: Location consumer uses core NATS, not a JetStream durable queue group

**Issue**: #142 (https://github.com/org/fleet-dispatch/issues/142)
**Type**: BUG
**Investigated**: 2026-06-08T18:42:00Z

### Assessment
| Metric | Value | Reasoning |
|---|---|---|
| Severity | HIGH | 3x redundant DB writes at 600 msg/s plus data-loss risk on simultaneous restart — production scaling + durability defect. |
| Complexity | MEDIUM | Localized to location_consumer.go and one service write path, but introduces a JetStream stream/consumer and a schema change. |
| Confidence | HIGH | Root cause directly observable: nats.Subscribe at location_consumer.go:31 with no queue group. |

## Problem Statement
Each of 3 replicas independently processes every fleet.driver.location message because the consumer uses core NATS `nats.Subscribe` with no queue group, tripling DB write load and losing messages on full restart. Updates also overwrite the driver row instead of appending history.

## Analysis
### Root Cause
- Why are messages processed 3x? Because all replicas get every message. (location_consumer.go:31)
- Why? `nc.Subscribe("fleet.driver.location", handler)` is a fan-out subscription, no queue group.
- Why no dedup? Core NATS, not JetStream — no durable consumer or queue group semantics.
- Why data-loss? Core NATS does not persist; nothing replays after restart.
- Why slow analytics? handler calls `driverSvc.UpsertLocation` which overwrites models.Driver.Location instead of appending history.
Actual code:
```go
// location_consumer.go:31
sub, err := nc.Subscribe("fleet.driver.location", func(m *nats.Msg) {
    c.handle(ctx, m.Data)
})
```
### Affected Files
| File | Lines | Action | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| events/location_consumer.go | 18-45 | UPDATE | Switch to JetStream durable queue group consumer |
| events/stream_setup.go | new | CREATE | Declare DRIVER_LOCATION stream |
| services/driver_service.go | UpsertLocation | UPDATE | Append history + refresh latest view |
| migrations/008_driver_location_history.sql | new | CREATE | history table + latest_location matview |
### Integration Points
Publisher events/publisher.go already targets fleet.driver.location — subject unchanged; only consumer changes.
### Git History
Introduced: commit a1b2c3 (initial consumer). Last modified: d4e5f6. Implication: predates multi-replica scale-out, so fan-out was acceptable for a single replica.

## Implementation Plan
### Step 1: Declare JetStream stream
- File events/stream_setup.go, Action CREATE
- Required: AddStream{Name:"DRIVER_LOCATION", Subjects:["fleet.driver.location"], Retention: Limits, MaxAge: 10m}
- Why: persistence + replay.
### Step 2: Durable queue group consumer
- File events/location_consumer.go:18-45, Action UPDATE
- Current:
```go
sub, err := nc.Subscribe("fleet.driver.location", func(m *nats.Msg){ c.handle(ctx, m.Data) })
```
- Required:
```go
js, _ := nc.JetStream()
sub, err := js.QueueSubscribe("fleet.driver.location", "dispatch-location-workers",
    func(m *nats.Msg){ if err := c.handle(ctx, m.Data); err == nil { m.Ack() } },
    nats.Durable("dispatch-location"), nats.ManualAck())
```
- Why: exactly-one replica per message + durable replay.
### Step 3: Append-only write path
- File services/driver_service.go UpsertLocation, Action UPDATE — INSERT into driver_location_history, then REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY latest_location. Keep handler idempotent (use message dedup id).
### Step 4: Add/Update Tests
- describe("location_consumer") it("acks only after successful handle"), it("only one of N workers handles a message"); describe("UpsertLocation") it("appends history row"), it("is idempotent for duplicate delivery").

## Patterns to Follow
```go
// SOURCE: events/publisher.go — existing JetStream context usage
js, err := nc.JetStream()
```

## Edge Cases & Risks
| Risk/Edge Case | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Duplicate redelivery on JetStream | Idempotent handler keyed on driver+timestamp |
| Matview refresh contention at 200/s | REFRESH ... CONCURRENTLY, or debounce refresh |
| Existing in-flight core subscription during deploy | Roll out stream first, then consumers |

## Validation
- `go test ./events/... ./services/...`
- Manual: scale to 3 replicas, publish 200 msg/s, confirm total DB writes ~200/s (not 600).

## Scope Boundaries
IN SCOPE: consumer change, stream declaration, history table + matview, idempotency.
OUT OF SCOPE: publisher changes, dashboard analytics queries (separate issue).

## Metadata
Investigated by: archon-investigate-issue · 2026-06-08T18:42:00Z · Artifact: $ARTIFACTS_DIR/investigation.md
```

# Investigation: TOCTOU race in class capacity check

**Issue**: #412 (https://github.com/org/gympass-access/issues/412)
**Type**: BUG
**Investigated**: 2026-06-08T14:40:00Z

### Assessment
| Metric | Value | Reasoning |
|---|---|---|
| Severity | HIGH | Over-booking corrupts capacity-1 classes; data-integrity label confirmed reproducible. |
| Complexity | LOW | Fix is wrapping existing count+insert in the already-present advisory lock. |
| Confidence | HIGH | Root cause is a confirmed non-atomic read-then-write with an unused lock path. |

## Problem Statement
Two concurrent bookings for the last seat both pass the capacity check because the count read and the insert are separate, unlocked statements. An advisory-lock code path exists but the booking API never enables it.

## Analysis
### Root Cause (5 Whys)
1. Why double-book? Both requests read count=0 before either inserts. (actions.ts:12-16)
2. Why? Count and insert are separate statements with no transaction lock. (actions.ts:12-30)
3. Why no lock? bookClass only locks when `useAdvisoryLock:true`. 
4. Why not passed? `route.ts:18` calls `bookClass(memberId, classId, gymId)` with no flag.
5. Why optional at all? Lock was retrofitted (commit a91f3c) behind a flag for backward-compat and never made default.
Primitive: capacity enforcement must be atomic per class. It is not. Minimal change: acquire `pg_try_advisory_xact_lock(classId)` around count+insert, unconditionally.

### Affected Files
| File | Lines | Action | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| src/features/bookings/actions.ts | 5-40 | UPDATE | wrap count+insert in withAdvisoryLock |
| src/app/api/bookings/route.ts | 18 | UPDATE | remove now-obsolete flag dependence |

### Integration Points
withAdvisoryLock (locks.ts:11-29) runs inside a transaction (xact lock auto-released on commit).
### Git History
Introduced a91f3c (2025-11-02); last modified d22e0b. Implication: regression window is all releases since Nov 2025.

## Implementation Plan
### Step 1: Make the lock mandatory
- File src/features/bookings/actions.ts, Lines 12-30, Action UPDATE
- Current: `const bookingCount = await db.select(...).where(and(eq(bookings.classId, classId), eq(bookings.status,'confirmed')));` then unlocked insert.
- Required: run count + capacity check + insert inside `return withAdvisoryLock(classId, async (tx) => { ... })`.
- Why: serializes concurrent bookings per class so only one passes the last-seat check.
### Step 2: Add/Update Tests
- describe('bookClass concurrency') it('rejects the second of two simultaneous last-seat bookings'); it('still filters by gym_id').

## Patterns to Follow
`// SOURCE: src/lib/locks.ts:11-29` withAdvisoryLock(key, fn) -> `pg_try_advisory_xact_lock`.

## Edge Cases & Risks
| Risk/Edge Case | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Lock contention under load | xact lock is short-lived; released on commit |
| Member already booked | unique (member_id,class_id) index check inside lock |

## Validation
`npm test bookings` and a concurrency test issuing two parallel bookClass calls expecting one success + one waitlist.

## Scope Boundaries
IN SCOPE: capacity atomicity, mandatory lock. OUT OF SCOPE: waitlist position race, gym_id filter bug (separate issues).
